name: Mercury
description: The smallest and closest planet to the Sun.
longDescription: Mercury is a rocky planet with a surface scarred by craters, looking much like Earth's Moon. It has almost no atmosphere to retain heat, leading to extreme temperature swings.

name: Venus
description: Earth's evil twin with a runaway greenhouse effect.
longDescription: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system. Its thick atmosphere traps heat, and it rains sulfuric acid, making it a truly volcanic and hostile world.

name: Earth
description: Our home planet and the only known world with life.
longDescription: Earth is an ocean world. Its unique atmosphere and liquid water make it the only place in the universe known to harbor life and complex ecosystems.

name: Mars
description: The Red Planet, home to the largest volcano in the solar system.
longDescription: Mars is a cold, desert world with a thin atmosphere. It is the focus of intense exploration for signs of ancient microbial life and potential colonization.

name: Jupiter
description: A gas giant twice as massive as all other planets combined.
longDescription: Jupiter is famous for its Great Red Spot, a giant storm larger than Earth. It lacks a solid surface and is composed mainly of hydrogen and helium.

name: Saturn
description: The jewel of the solar system, known for its dazzling rings.
longDescription: Saturn is a gas giant with the most extensive ring system in the solar system, made of billions of small chunks of ice and rock.

name: Uranus
description: An ice giant that rotates on its side.
longDescription: Uranus has a unique tilt, making it appear to roll around the Sun. It is an extremely cold world with a faint ring system and 27 known moons.

name: Neptune
description: The windiest world, dark, cold, and whipped by supersonic winds.
longDescription: Neptune is the most distant major planet. It is 17 times the mass of Earth and has a thick atmosphere that gives it a beautiful deep blue color.

name: Ceres
description: The largest object in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter.
longDescription: Ceres is a dwarf planet that contains a significant amount of water ice. It was the first dwarf planet visited by a spacecraft (Dawn).

name: Pluto
description: An icy world with mountains, valleys, and glaciers.
longDescription: Once the ninth planet, Pluto is now the most famous dwarf planet. It has a complex surface and a thin atmosphere of nitrogen and methane.

name: Haumea
description: A fast-spinning, football-shaped world in the Kuiper Belt.
longDescription: Haumea rotates so fast that it has been stretched into an ellipsoid. It is one of the fastest rotating large objects in our solar system.

name: Makemake
description: A reddish world covered in frozen ethane and methane.
longDescription: Makemake is the second brightest object in the Kuiper Belt after Pluto. It lacks a substantial atmosphere and is extremely cold.

name: Eris
description: One of the most massive dwarf planets, once thought to be the 10th planet.
longDescription: Eris is a very distant, icy dwarf planet. Its discovery triggered the debate that led to the new definition of a planet and the reclassification of Pluto.
